name: Systems Engineering Tradeoff Lens
summary: A comparison tool for weighing performance, cost, risk, time, and human factors inside Systems Engineering.
whenToUseIt: Use this when two options both look attractive until you make the tradeoffs explicit.
steps: Clarify the decision and the objective.
Compare the available options against those dimensions.
Decide what you are optimizing for and what you are willing to absorb.
Record the rationale so the choice can be revisited later.
example: Use it on a rail signaling project to compare speed, reliability, and strategic fit.
